figures.caption: Figure 1: (a) Flight envelope for the NASA-CRM configuration in the M-𝛼 plane. (b) Representative 𝐶𝑝 distribution over the upper surface of the wing, corresponding to the flight condition vector (M, 𝛼, 𝛿1,𝛿2, 𝛿3, 𝛿4)= (0.6, 2.5◦,−8.6◦,−8.0◦,−6.36◦,−1.38◦).
Figure 4: Chordwise pressure distributions for actual and surrogate 𝐶𝑝 fields across span percentages 𝜂 = 0.3, 0.6, 0.9. The position over the airfoil is normalized with the chord 𝑐.
Figure 9: (a) LRI curves as a function of 𝜔 and the Mach number associated with the flight condition. (b) GRI-MAEI relationship.
Table 3: Hyperparameters and architectural specifications of the proposed DDPM framework.

Figure 3: MAE distribution between actual and predicted 𝐶𝑝 fields for the different models and database sets. Visualization test cases are denoted by symbols. Dashed lines represent the global mean MAE for each model. Note that the distribution tails are truncated at the sample extrema (minimum and maximum observed errors).

Figure 8: Spatial deviation of the model. The rows display the actual fields (x) along with the ensemble means (𝝁𝜃 ) and standard deviations (𝝈𝜃 ) computed from 𝑁 = 1000 independent samples for each flight condition.
Figure 6: Visualization test cases together with the three maximum- and minimum-error flight conditions for the DDPM-S within the training and testing CH spaces.
Figure 7: Convergence of the model’s statistics as a function of ensemble size𝑁 . Bar symbol denotes mean values across the 𝑙 flight conditions.
Figure 2: (a) Training process of the surrogate. (b) Conditional sample generation from Gaussian noise.
Figure 5: Error distribution over the upper surface of the wing for DDPM-S (top row), and MLP (bottom row) models.
